data.title: OpenAI explored ad sales with The Trade Desk as revenue hits $25B: The ⁠Information
data.description:
• OpenAI (OPENAI) has held early talks to partner with The Trade Desk (TTD) to help it sell advertising, as it leans on external partners to ramp up its business, The Information reported on Wednesday.
  • TTD (TTD) shares rose 9.3% in Wednesday’s after-market session; ad-tech peer AppLovin’s (APP) stock rose more than 1%. OpenAI is also reportedly in talks with other brands, media agencies, and ad tech firms as part of the early ads pilot.
  • The Sam Altman-led company has recently said that it was beginning to test ads in ChatGPT in the U.S. The test will be for logged-in adult users on the Free and Go subscription tiers. 
  • According to a separate report, the artificial intelligence startup topped $25 billion in annualized revenue as of the ‌end of last month. This is a ​17% increase from the $21.4 billion in annualized revenue that the company generated at the end of the year, the report said.

data.title: Meta eyes in-house chips to train AI models - report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) plans to expand its custom silicon efforts beyond applications for simple recommendation algorithms to eventually support complex AI model training, according to a Bloomberg report that cites the company CFO's comments from a recent conference.

The company believes some of its workloads, especially those tied to AI models and personalized recommendations, are customized, and building its own chips could improve performance and efficiency. The strategy aims to tailor hardware to Meta's (META) unique needs, reducing reliance on third-party suppliers like Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD).

“Some of our workloads really are very customized to us,” Meta CFO Susan Li commented at a technology conference. “The sort of ranking and recommendations workloads have been where we have started, and that’s the place where we have rolled out custom silicon at the most scale. But we expect and are hopeful that we are going to expand that over time, including eventually to training AI models.” he added.

The testing of Meta's (META) first in-house AI training chip began in early 2025, and executives had earlier targeted 2026 rollout for generative AI, though its advanced designs like Olympus faced setbacks in February.

Still, Meta (META) prioritizes proprietary silicon for customized workloads, with the move aligning with broader industry trends to optimize AI infrastructure costs and performance.

data.title: China signals era of 'sober growth' sets lowest GDP target since 1991 at 4.5%–5%
data.description:
At the opening of Thursday’s National People’s Congress, Premier Li Qiang unveiled a GDP growth target of 4.5% to 5% for 2026. This modest goal signals a definitive new chapter of "sober growth," marking the most restrained economic target Beijing has set since the early 1990s as it prioritizes structural stability over rapid expansion.

This cautious forecast indicates that policymakers are more open to accepting slower growth while focusing on sustainable economic drivers and reducing dependence on debt for property and infrastructure.

During the annual legislative session, Premier Li Qiang stated the government wants to maintain growth momentum after achieving its 2025 target, aided by strong exports despite trade issues with the U. S.

It acknowledges that the growth model of the past 40 years is under pressure. 

Meanwhile, the budget deficit will remain at around 4% of GDP, unchanged from 2025, with a consumer inflation target of around 2.0% to prevent deflation and to support growth amid domestic challenges and rising geopolitical risks. 

Stabilizing employment remains a priority; the government aims to create 12 million urban jobs and keep the unemployment rate at 5.5%, unchanged from 2025. 

In addition, local governments will issue CNY 4.4 trillion in special-purpose bonds for investments, and CNY 1.3 trillion in treasury bonds will also be issued to reinforce fiscal support.

Separately, China pledged to stabilize its property sector and optimize housing supply, according to an official government report cited by Reuters. Authorities plan to better utilize existing commodity housing stock, including purchasing unsold homes and converting them into government-subsidized housing to reduce excess inventory.

On the external front, mainland stocks tracked a rebound in global markets as inflationary concerns eased, though hostilities between the US and Iran persisted.

On Thursday, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.5% to around 4,100, while the Shenzhen Component gained 1.5% to 14,130 on Thursday, breaking a two-day decline, and the offshore yuan steadied around 6.89 per dollar on Thursday, as a stronger daily fixing helped offset cautious market sentiment following Beijing’s newly lowered economic growth target. 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

data.title: Venezuela’s state‑owned miner signs deal to sell 1,000 kg of gold to Trafigura; eyes U.S. mining cooperation
data.description:
Venezuela's state-owned mining company on Monday inked a multimillion-dollar deal to sell as many as 1,000 kilograms of gold destined for U.S. markets, Axios reported on Wednesday. The deal requires Venezuelan mining company ​Minerven to furnish ​650 to 1,000 kg of gold ‌dore ⁠bars to Trafigura, the report said, adding that Trafigura will ship the gold to ​U.S. ​refineries ⁠under a separate arrangement with the U.S. ​government.

The contract calls for 98% final gold content, the source said. U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, who arrived in Venezuela Wednesday to discuss oil and mineral opportunities, helped shepherd the gold contract.

Gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) have already climbed nearly 20% this year, after hitting a record high above $5,595 per ounce in late January, as global uncertainty and safe-haven demand kept bullion markets firm.

This contract is the third extraction deal made under the Trump administration's supervision as the U.S. has taken control of Venezuela's most crucial and abundant resource, oil, the report said. Trafigura is also involved in those oil contracts worth more than $1 billion.

Separatetly, Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Wednesday a reform of the country's main mining law will be submitted in coming days to the country's legislature, after a ​meeting in Caracas with Burgum.

Burgum, who also heads the U.S. National Energy Dominance Council, arrived in the South American country with more than two dozen U.S. mining and minerals companies, which he said could represent billions in investment and thousands of high-paying jobs for Venezuela. Burgum is expected to meet with oil and gas ​companies on Thursday to discuss expansion and ​investment, two sources earlier told Reuters.

data.title: Australia's trade surplus shrinks to AUD 2.63B in January, missing forecasts as export momentum fades
data.description:
Australia’s goods trade surplus dropped to AUD 2.63 billion in January 2026, down from AUD 3.37 billion in December and below the expected AUD 3.9 billion.

This decline was due to lower exports, which fell 0.9% to AUD 44.06 billion, and a 0.8% rise in imports to AUD 41.43 billion, indicating stronger domestic demand at the start of the year.

In Australia, the latest data showed household spending rebounded slightly in January after falling in December, indicating softer consumer demand. Australia's trade surplus shrinks to AUD 2.63B in January, missing forecasts 

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.4% to around 8,900 in Thursday morning deals, after shedding more than 3% over the previous two sessions. The Australian dollar hovered around $0.706, trading in a narrow range near three-year peaks, as investors weighed the impact and duration of the ongoing Middle East conflict

More on Australia:

data.title: U.S. Senate votes to back Trump on Iran strikes
data.description:
The U.S. Senate voted to block a Democratic-led resolution that would have restricted President Donald Trump's authority to conduct further military strikes on Iran without congressional approval. The action aligns with Republican support for Trump's ongoing campaign against Iran amid escalating U.S.-Israeli operations.

The Senate voted 53 to 47 not to advance the resolution. Nearly all Republicans opposed the measure, with only Sen. Rand Paul joining Democrats in favor; Sen. John Fetterman was the sole Democrat against it.

The Democrats cautioned that the administration has bypassed Congress and risked drawing the U.S. into a broader conflict without proper oversight. While the Republicans mainly defended Trump’s actions, calling them legal and necessary for U.S. national security and within the President’s authority as commander-in-chief.

"This is not a forever war, indeed not even close to it. This is going to end very quickly," Republican Senator Jim Risch of Idaho, chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, said in a speech against the resolution.

The vote comes amid an expanding conflict with Iran and Trump's airstrikes on Iranian targets, including nuclear sites and leadership, as part of "Operation Epic Fury" launched days earlier with Israel.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Thursday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:30 AM Challenger Job-Cut Report 

This monthly report counts and categorizes announcements of corporate layoffs based on mass layoff data from state departments of labor. 

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims seen at 215K, up from 212K last week.

8:30 AM Productivity and Costs 

Productivity measures the growth of labor efficiency in producing the economy's goods and services. 

The delayed first reading for Q4 is expected to show much slower productivity growth at 1.9 percent versus 4.9 percent in Q3 and ULC up 2.1 percent versus a decrease of 1.9 percent in Q3.

8:30 AM Import and Export Prices

Import price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods that are bought in the United States but produced abroad, and export price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods sold abroad but produced domestically. 

Import prices are seen up 0.1 percent and export prices up 0.2 percent in January on the month.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country.

1:15 PM Michelle Bowman Speaks

Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman speaks before the virtual New York Bankers Association "Navigating What's Next: Perspective on the Economy and Innovation" event.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Elon Musk says he paid full price for Twitter due to ‘biased’ Delaware judge
data.description:
Elon Musk testified he had to pay full price to buy Twitter in 2022 because the Delaware judge in the company’s lawsuit to make him consummate the $44 billion deal was “biased” against him, Bloomberg News reported.

Musk has openly complained before about Delaware Chancery Court’s chief judge, Kathaleen St. J. McCormick. But his testimony at a San Francisco trial revisiting the tumultuous buyout marked the first time the world’s richest person has publicly said why he ultimately decided to back down in his fight with Twitter, the report added.

Musk said his lawyers warned him he was unlikely to beat the suit after McCormick issued pretrial rulings favoring Twitter. In a separate case, McCormick twice voided Musk’s Tesla (TSLA) pay package, which the Delaware Supreme Court reinstated in December 2025. 

“The lawyers said there was no choice,” Musk told jurors, explaining why he ultimately agreed to close the deal after months of dispute. The testimony came during a trial examining claims from Twitter investors that Musk misled markets during the turbulent takeover process.

According to Bloomberg, the investors argue Musk’s comments — including posts suggesting the deal was temporarily “on hold” — influenced trading behaviour and caused financial losses for some shareholders.

One investor, Brian Belgrave, told the court he sold thousands of Twitter shares in July 2022 after concluding Musk would abandon the takeover. Belgrave later watched Musk complete the acquisition at $54.20 per share, a price significantly higher than the amount he had received when selling his holdings. “I got cheated,” Belgrave said in testimony cited by Bloomberg.

In March 2025, xAI acquired Twitter (now X) in an all-stock deal that valued the social media platform at about $33 billion.

Musk's X competes with other popular social media firms such as Meta's (META) Facebook and Instagram, Google's YouTube, Snap's (SNAP) Snapchat, TikTok, Pinterest (PINS), Microsoft's (MSFT) LinkedIn, and Reddit (RDDT).

data.title: Viemed Healthcare reports mixed Q4 results; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Viemed Healthcare press release (VMD): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.14 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $76.2M (+25.5% Y/Y) misses by $1.15M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025 totaled $18.2 million and a record $61.4 million, respectively.

  • The Company continued to generate strong free cash flow while delivering robust growth.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities for the year ended December 31, 2025 totaled $51.9 million compared with $39.1 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

  • Free cash flow for the year ended December 31, 2025 totaled $28.1 million compared with $11.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

  • The Company's ventilator patient count totaled 12,259 as of December 31, 2025, an increase of 4% over December 31, 2024.

  • Full Year 2026 Guidance (all dollar amounts are USD):

    The Company is providing the following financial guidance for the year ending December 31, 2026:

    • Net revenue is expected to be in the range of $310 million to $320 million vs. consensus of $328.73M.

    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $65 million to $69 million.

    • Net capital expenditures are expected to be in the range of 10% to 11.5% of net revenue.

data.title: Stevanato Group outlines 2026 revenue target up to €1.308B with high-value solutions set to reach 48% of sales
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stevanato Group (STVN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Franco Stevanato, CEO & Executive Chairman, reported that "for the fiscal 2025, total company revenue increased by 9% at constant-currency rates and 7% on a reported basis compared with 2024." He highlighted double-digit top-line growth in the Biopharmaceutical and Diagnostic Solutions (BDS) segment, noting that "high-value solutions... increased 29% in fiscal 2025 and represented 46% of the total company revenue for the year." The CEO emphasized the company's focus on expanding capacity for high-value syringes, particularly the Nexa syringe, which was the fastest-growing product in 2025, driven by GLP-1 demand.
  • Stevanato stated that "in 2025, the Nexa syringe was our fastest-growing product, driven primarily by growth from GLP-1s... our revenue from GLP-1s accounted for approximately 19% to 20% of total company revenue, growing more than 50% compared with 2024."
  • He also discussed ongoing investments in Fishers, Indiana and Latina, Italy, aimed at expanding capacity for high-value solutions, and indicated a pivot away from non-high-value product categories.
  • Marco Dal Lago, CFO, stated, "total Company revenue grew 7% at constant currency and 5% on a reported basis to EUR 346.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2025." Dal Lago cited a "13% at constant currency and 10% on a reported basis" revenue increase in the BDS segment and a "23% revenue decline in the Engineering Segment." He noted that "gross profit margin increased 120 basis points to 30.9%" in Q4, with net profit at EUR 47.6 million and diluted EPS at EUR 0.17. Adjusted EBITDA rose to EUR 97.7 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 28.2%.

Outlook

  • The company established 2026 guidance with expected revenue in the range of EUR 1.260 billion to EUR 1.290 billion, or up to EUR 1.308 billion on a constant-currency basis. Adjusted EBITDA is expected between EUR 331.8 million and EUR 346.9 million, and adjusted diluted EPS between EUR 0.59 and EUR 0.63.
  • Dal Lago indicated, "For the full year, the BDS Segment is expected to grow on a reported basis high-single to low double digits and double digits on a constant-currency rate. Engineering is expected to decline by mid-single digit to low double digits."
  • The company projects high-value solutions to represent "between 47% to 48% of total Company revenue" in 2026, with revenue expected to be stronger in the second half of the year.
  • Expected CapEx for 2026 is between EUR 270 million and EUR 290 million before customer contributions, with free cash flow modeled at breakeven to approximately EUR 20 million.

Financial Results

  • For the fourth quarter, revenue from high-value solutions grew 31% to EUR 171 million, making up approximately 49% of total revenue. BDS segment revenue reached EUR 307.1 million, with gross profit margin for BDS improving to 31.6%.
  • Engineering segment revenue fell to EUR 39.4 million, with gross profit margin at 15.8%.
  • Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were EUR 130.6 million, with net debt of EUR 337.7 million. Full year capital expenditures totaled EUR 294.9 million, and free cash flow was EUR 18.4 million for 2025.

Q&A

  • Michael Ryskin, BofA Securities: Asked about GLP-1 growth expectations for 2026. Franco Stevanato replied, "we think that will be a growth in the range of mid-teens."
  • Ryskin followed up on the Engineering segment order intake. Stevanato explained, "the sales cycle because of the technicality of this line is take a little bit longer that was expected... the outlook in the medium term is strong also in the Engineering division."
  • Patrick Donnelly, Citi: Asked about high-value solutions capacity. Stevanato stated, "practically, we run approximately full capacity in Stevanato Group... the ramping up that we are doing in Latina... and Fishers."
  • Donnelly asked about margin expansion for 2026. Dal Lago responded, "we see margin expansion from 0 to 30 basis points on consolidated level... gross profit margin, we can put together some headwinds and tailwinds."
  • Douglas Schenkel, Wolfe Research: Asked about long-term GLP-1 growth and economics. Stevanato indicated, "GLP-1 in the next decade, it will continue to be a powerful tailwind for Stevanato."
  • David Windley, Jefferies: Clarified the definition of GLP-1s and asked about the role of Nexa syringes in 2026. Stevanato confirmed that "Nexa syringes will continue to play an important role in the GLP-1s" in 2026.
  • Paul Knight, KeyBanc: Asked about the rationale for mid-teens GLP-1s growth guidance. Stevanato described it as "realistic" due to "a massive preparation of the supply-chain" in 2025.
  • Kallum Titchmarsh, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about non-GLP biologics. Stevanato discussed focus areas including monoclonal antibodies, immunology, and rare diseases, and their impact on high-value product demand.
  • Matthew Larew, William Blair: Asked about the split between injectables and orals for GLP-1s. Stevanato indicated injectables will remain the majority at around 70%.
  • Curtis Moiles, BNP Paribas: Sought clarification on the shift away from non-high-value solutions. Stevanato stated, "we are going maybe to deprioritize the ampules... the goal is to focus to become the #1, #2 in the new product."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a mix of curiosity and skepticism, especially around the Engineering segment's slower order intake and the rationale for mid-teens GLP-1s growth after 50% growth in 2025. Questions pressed for specifics on capacity, margin expansion, and product mix.
  • Management maintained an overall confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing strong demand and strategic execution, but shifted to a more cautious and explanatory stance during Q&A on Engineering and capacity constraints. Phrases such as "we think that will be a growth in the range of mid-teens" and "we are going maybe to deprioritize the ampules" signaled realistic rather than optimistic expectations.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed slightly increased caution on Engineering, while confidence on high-value solutions and GLP-1s persisted.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more detailed, with explicit revenue and margin targets and a clearly stated expectation for mid-teens GLP-1 growth, compared to broader optimism in Q3.
  • The strategic focus continues to shift toward high-value solutions, with further deprioritization of legacy products and non-strategic categories.
  • Management's confidence on BDS and GLP-1s remains high, but the Engineering segment is acknowledged as a continuing challenge, with more conservative growth expectations and a slower order conversion cycle.
  • Analysts' questions shifted from inventory normalization and capacity ramp-up to more granular inquiries about product mix, segment margins, and the sustainability of recent growth rates.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing headwinds from foreign currency translation and tariffs, with the latter being largely transferred to customers.
  • Engineering segment order intake remains lower than anticipated, attributed to longer sales cycles and operational focus.
  • Capacity constraints on high-value solutions are acknowledged, with the company running at full capacity in key product lines and ongoing investments to address demand.
  • Analyst concerns centered on the sustainability of GLP-1s growth, the ability to expand capacity fast enough, and the timeline for Engineering margin recovery.

Final Takeaway

Stevanato Group's management highlighted robust progress in high-value solutions and biologics, leading to significant margin expansion and strengthening of its market position, especially in GLP-1s and premium injectable products. Looking to 2026, the company expects continued revenue growth, with high-value solutions projected to reach up to 48% of total sales and mid-teens growth in GLP-1s. While the Engineering segment faces ongoing challenges, operational improvements and a healthy pipeline support the outlook for recovery. Overall, strategic investments and a focus on premium offerings position Stevanato for sustained growth and increased participation in both established and emerging biologics markets.
